title: Distinction Primitive
statement: A distinction is an operation or relation that separates at least two alternatives within a possibility space.
failure_condition: Not falsified in usual sense; usefulness can fail.

title: Boundary Inheritance
statement: Once a system distinguishes itself from what it is not, it inherits a boundary.
failure_condition: Bounded system with zero maintenance cost under sustained load.

title: Finite Capacity
statement: A finite system with a boundary has finite representational and operational capacity.
failure_condition: Physically instantiated bounded system with infinite operational capacity.

title: Capacity Deficit
statement: When task-relevant distinction demand exceeds accessible capacity, the system operates under a capacity deficit.
failure_condition: Not directly falsifiable; usefulness can fail.

title: Budget Exits
statement: A finite system under persistent positive capacity deficit must prune, externalize, relax the task, compress, or collapse.
not_claimed: Does not prescribe which exit occurs.
failure_condition: System under sustained deficit with none of the listed exits.

title: Invariant-Supported Persistence
statement: Systems that persist under finite capacity do so by maintaining invariants that reduce effective distinction load.
not_claimed: Does not identify which invariants are used.
failure_condition: Persistent system under sustained deficit with no invariant-supported load reduction.

title: Finite Observer Projection
statement: A finite physical observer O can operationally use only a finite image Im(pi_O) of a physical possibility space.
dependencies: Finite record carrier; finite memory; finite error tolerance
not_claimed: Not consciousness; not quantum measurement solution; not unlimited access.
failure_condition: Observer with unbounded distinctions under finite resources.

title: Distinguishability Budget
statement: Operational distinguishability is bounded by minimum of internal record capacity and accessible boundary/channel capacity.
failure_condition: Observer using more distinctions than any capacity bound allows.

title: Stock vs Throughput
statement: Accessible capacity separates into stock capacity and update throughput; effective task capacity is their minimum.
failure_condition: Observer exceeding both stock and throughput bounds.

title: Boundary-Relative Capacity Deficit
statement: Delta_FDS = R_min - C_acc where R_min is task demand and C_acc is accessible capacity.
failure_condition: Not falsified directly; usefulness can fail.

title: Budget-Exit Theorem
statement: If Delta_FDS > 0 persists, observer must enter at least one exit class.
failure_condition: Full-fidelity task under sustained deficit with no exit.

title: Maintenance Inequality
statement: Positive deficit implies Landauer-style lower bound on thermodynamic maintenance cost for irreversible erasure.
not_claimed: Lower bound only; not full thermodynamic model.
failure_condition: Irreversible erasure below Landauer cost.

title: Budget-Crossing Signature
statement: As chi = R_min - C_acc crosses zero, observers should show measurable transitions.
failure_condition: No transition under controlled budget crossing.

title: Bottleneck-Switching Kink
statement: Rate-distortion error floor shows slope discontinuities at bottleneck switches.
failure_condition: Smooth error floor with no measurable slope change.

title: Residue-Pruning-Boundary Loop
statement: Sustained flux generates residue; residue impairs function; pruning controls residue.
not_claimed: Not all biological persistence follows this loop.
failure_condition: Protocell-like system with sustained flux and no pruning that persists indefinitely.

title: Active Pruning Threshold
statement: There exists a critical pruning rate S_c below which residue cannot be bounded.
failure_condition: Persistent system with pruning below S_c and no equivalent mechanism.

title: Maintenance-Attractor Loss
statement: Below threshold pruning, the system crosses a saddle-node fold and loses stability.
failure_condition: Collapse without fold-like slowing.

title: Rescue-Window Closure
statement: Restoring pruning rescues system only within a finite delay window.
failure_condition: Pruning restoration always rescues independent of delay.

title: Spatial Clogging
statement: Residue accumulation causes local clogging and boundary deformation.
not_claimed: Not all systems exhibit spatial clogging.

title: Radius-Dependent Pruning Demand
statement: Required pruning increases with system radius in spatial protocell models.

title: Reportability as Finite-Capacity Maintenance
statement: Conscious reportability can be modeled as a maintained finite-capacity regime.
failure_condition: Sustained reportable access under deficit with no maintenance mechanisms.

title: Representational Residue
statement: Unresolved rate-distortion surplus accumulates as representational residue.

title: Attention as Capacity-Limited Distinction Admission
statement: Attention is capacity-limited distinction admission into an update channel.
failure_condition: Attention-like selection occurs without finite capacity, admission, update gating, or priority constraint under the specified mapping.

title: Salience and Attention Are Separable
statement: Salience and attention are separable. Salient distinctions can be rejected if cost or verification burden is too high.
failure_condition: Empirical systems always admit highest-salience items regardless of cost, capacity, task, or verification constraints.

title: Boundary-Efficient Attention Prefers High Causal Value
statement: Boundary-efficient or loss-minimizing attention systems preferentially admit high causal boundary-value distinctions under controlled capacity conditions.
failure_condition: Admission patterns are no better predicted by causal boundary value than by raw salience or noise under a valid mapping.

title: Attention Allocation as Constrained Optimization
statement: Attention allocation can be written as constrained optimization over value, curiosity, cost, and capacity.
failure_condition: No useful mapping exists between admission patterns and constrained allocation variables.

title: Deficit Steepens Admission Thresholds and Produces Tunnel Vision
statement: Semantic or attention deficit steepens admission thresholds and can produce tunnel vision.
failure_condition: High load or deficit produces no narrowing, thresholding, or priority collapse in systems claimed to have finite attention.

title: Artificial Attention Requires Coupled Architecture
statement: Artificial attention belongs to a coupled architecture only when routed distinctions affect durable update, action, maintenance, or verification.
failure_condition: Bare attention weights alone satisfy strong FDS attention without durable update or downstream relevance.

title: Collective Attention as Shared Admission
statement: Collective attention is shared admission under finite communication, verification, and externalized memory capacity.
failure_condition: Group-scale attention shows no relation to verification capacity, agenda-setting, or externalized memory.

title: Attention Failure Modes as Admission Errors
statement: Attention failure includes overload, distraction, salience capture, suppression, tunnel vision, false admission, and critical distinction exclusion.
failure_condition: These failure modes cannot be operationalized as admission errors under finite capacity.

title: Attention Recovery Hysteresis
statement: Attention recovery after deficit-induced narrowing can lag behind external load reduction because of hysteresis in gate thresholds, verification routines, or maintained threat priors.
failure_condition: Attention gates relax immediately and without lag after load reduction in systems where hysteresis is claimed.

title: FDS-Value as Causal Boundary-Gradient Relevance
statement: FDS-value is causal boundary-gradient relevance under a specified boundary, loss, intervention grammar, horizon, and cost model.
failure_condition: Valuation cannot be operationalized as causal effect on any specified future boundary-maintenance loss under valid mappings.

title: Predictive Relevance and Causal FDS-Value Are Separable
statement: Predictive relevance and causal FDS-value are separable.
failure_condition: Correlational predictors always coincide with intervention-relevant boundary effects under audited systems.

title: Value Ranking as Finite-Difference Ordering
statement: Value ranking can be expressed as an ordering over finite-difference action, admission, maintenance, or policy effects.
failure_condition: No useful ordering exists between evaluands and their causal boundary effects under stated mappings.

title: Risk-Weighted Value Can Dominate Average-Loss Value
statement: Near collapse thresholds, risk-weighted FDS-value can dominate average-loss value.
failure_condition: Collapse-risk reduction never changes ranking near boundary failure thresholds under valid mappings.

title: Goals as Stabilized FDS-Value Rankings
statement: Goals are stabilized FDS-value rankings coupled to policy orientation across update windows.
failure_condition: Goal-like behavior persists without ranking stability, memory, policy orientation, or update-window persistence.

title: Value Drift under Evaluation Deficit
statement: Value drift occurs when rankings change faster than the system can verify, update, or maintain the reasons for the change.
failure_condition: Ranking instability produces no detectable change in behavior, loss, or policy under claimed goal systems.

title: Proxy Reward Can Diverge from Causal Boundary Value
statement: Proxy reward can diverge from causal boundary value, creating reward hacking or misalignment.
failure_condition: Proxy optimization remains aligned despite divergent finite-difference effects on host boundary loss.

title: Collective Goals as Shared Stabilized Rankings
statement: Collective goals are shared stabilized rankings under finite verification and coordination capacity.
failure_condition: Group goals show no relation to shared rankings, institutional memory, verification capacity, or policy orientation.

title: Goal Recovery and Hysteresis
statement: Goal recovery can lag after resource or threat recovery because rankings, commitments, or threat priors persist.
failure_condition: Goals relax immediately and without lag after boundary load changes in systems where goal hysteresis is claimed.

title: FDS-Meaning as Actionable Semantic Quotient
statement: FDS-meaning is actionable semantic quotient under a specified system, boundary, task family, context family, policy or verification target, horizon, loss, tolerance, and capacity budget.
failure_condition: Compressed representations function semantically without preserving any action, prediction, verification, coordination, or boundary-relevant structure.

title: Semantic Quotient Must Preserve Policy-Relevant Distinctions
statement: A semantic quotient must preserve policy-relevant distinctions within tolerance.
failure_condition: Quotient classes systematically merge distinctions requiring different actions or updates under the audited task.

title: Semantic Compression Is Useful When It Reduces Load without Excess Loss
statement: Semantic compression is useful when it lowers capacity load without increasing boundary loss beyond tolerance.
failure_condition: Compression always degrades performance or never reduces maintained semantic load under valid mappings.

title: Semantic Deficit Produces Degradation
statement: Semantic deficit produces merging, loss, drift, unsupported completion, false compression, or meaning collapse.
failure_condition: Semantic overload produces no degradation, merging, proxy substitution, or action-relevance loss.

title: Embedding Similarity Is Not Sufficient for FDS-Meaning
statement: Embedding similarity is not sufficient for FDS-meaning unless it preserves downstream policy or verification structure.
failure_condition: Embedding-near items always remain policy-equivalent under audited tasks.

title: Shared Meaning Requires Quotient Synchronization
statement: Shared meaning requires synchronized semantic quotients and verification channels across agents.
failure_condition: Collective meaning persists without shared quotient, external record, translation, verification, or coordination channel.

title: Meaning Recovery Requires Quotient Reconstruction
statement: Meaning recovery requires reconstructing lost action-relevant distinctions, not merely increasing information volume.
failure_condition: Restoring raw information always restores task meaning without quotient reconstruction.

title: High-Level Meanings as Invariant Semantic Quotients
statement: High-level meanings are candidate invariant semantic quotients stable across contexts and perturbations.
dependencies: FDS-M3-001; M2 high-level goals invariant model
failure_condition: High-level meanings fail to preserve policy, value, or coordination relevance across any stated context family.

title: Non-Injective Truncation Creates Preimage Uncertainty
statement: Non-injective truncation creates preimage uncertainty relative to the effective record.
failure_condition: A many-to-one map contains enough information, without side records or conventions, to distinguish all of its preimages.

title: Bayes-Optimal Exact Recovery Bound
statement: Bayes-optimal guaranteed exact preimage recovery is bounded by the largest conditional preimage mass.
failure_condition: A decoder using only Z exceeds the Bayes-optimal classifier bound for X|Z.

title: Informational Hysteresis
statement: Capacity recovery does not recover distinctions erased during a bottleneck.
failure_condition: A finite system recovers exact task-relevant preimage distinctions after capacity restoration with no side record, no enlarged boundary, no external trace, and no hidden convention.

title: Non-Lumpability Creates Hidden-State Memory
statement: Non-lumpable coarse-graining creates hidden-state memory and effective stochasticity.
failure_condition: A non-lumpable projection closes exactly on Z_t alone without hidden state, history, or extra variables.

title: Mori-Zwanzig Memory Burden
statement: Projection-induced memory burden has a Mori-Zwanzig analogue.
failure_condition: Eliminated variables never reappear as memory, noise, or closure error in projected dynamics, even when lumpability fails.

title: Externalization Restores Inverse Information Only by Moving It
statement: Externalization restores inverse information only by moving it to a side ledger.
failure_condition: External logs restore exact recovery at no writing, retention, indexing, synchronization, retrieval, verification, or boundary-expansion cost.

title: Finite-Memory Exit Theorem
statement: Sustained truncation requires residual irrecoverability, side records, externalization, task relaxation, or failure.
failure_condition: A finite system repeatedly applies non-injective truncation to task-relevant distinctions while preserving exact recovery with no residual uncertainty and no extra ledger.

title: Invariant Side-Ledgers Suppress Residual Uncertainty
statement: Invariant side-ledgers can suppress P4 residual inverse uncertainty.
failure_condition: A task variable factors through an accessible invariant, but H(V|Z,Q_inv) remains high under the stated assumptions.

title: Noisy Invariant Recovery Bound
statement: Noisy invariant readout gives a bounded recovery penalty.
failure_condition: A noisy invariant readout with error probability δ exceeds the Fano-style bound without hidden information or changed task labels.

title: Local Perturbations Cannot Change Protected Invariant
statement: Local perturbations cannot change a protected invariant without a protection-breaking event.
dependencies: FDS-P7-001; local perturbation family; protection margin
failure_condition: A local perturbation changes the invariant while the protection gap, locality assumptions, and accounting boundary remain intact.

title: NHSE as Model Class
statement: NHSE supplies a model class for invariant-supported persistence.
failure_condition: NHSE is present, but it carries no stable recoverable distinction, no boundary-sensitive protection, and no robustness to local perturbation in the registered model class.

title: Protection Relocates Entropy/Resource Accounting
statement: Protection relocates entropy/resource accounting rather than deleting it.
failure_condition: A protected invariant supplies indefinite maintenance with no drive, boundary, refresh, dissipation, verification, control, or external ledger.

title: Dual-Channel Signature
statement: Protected phases can generate a dual forgetting/ledger signature.
failure_condition: Confirmed protection-breaking transition with no feature in operational forgetting and no corresponding resource/entropy signature under a well-powered registered protocol.

title: Boundary Maintenance Requires Finite Update Throughput
statement: Boundary maintenance requires finite update throughput.
failure_condition: A time-varying boundary is maintained without updating, verifying, storing, externalizing, protecting, or acting on any task-relevant distinction.

title: Speed and Precision Jointly Increase Maintenance Burden
statement: Speed and precision jointly increase maintenance burden.

title: Sustainable Internal Rate Is Bottlenecked
statement: The sustainable internal rate is bottlenecked by sensing, updating, verification, correction, action, and resources.
failure_condition: A system exceeds its slowest internal channel indefinitely without queueing, latency, loss, externalization, or resource expansion.

title: Correction and Verification Belong in the Resource Ledger
statement: Correction and verification belong in the resource ledger.
failure_condition: Physical correction, refresh, verification, synchronization, overwrite, and recovery are cost-free under the stated implementation assumptions.

title: Effective Causal Update Bandwidth Limits Real-Time Maintenance
statement: Effective causal update bandwidth limits real-time maintenance.
failure_condition: A finite observer integrates arbitrarily distant boundary-relevant information within a finite update window with no latency, no prediction burden, and no effective signal-speed limit.

title: Externalization and Invariant Compression Are Relief Channels, Not Free Exits

failure_condition: External ledgers or invariant quotients reduce internal demand with no write, synchronization, verification, protection, latency, or boundary-accounting cost.

title: Throughput Deficit Exit Theorem

failure_condition: Demand exceeds sustainable throughput with no exit channel and no boundary failure, given a valid implementation mapping.

title: Horizon as Boundary
statement: Cosmological horizons act as finite distinguishability boundaries for observers.
not_claimed: Not deriving GR or quantum gravity.
failure_condition: Horizon with no distinguishability constraint.

title: Horizon-Maintenance Scale
statement: Horizon-maintenance cost has scale rho ~ H^2 M_Pl^2, consistent with dark energy.
failure_condition: Dark energy unrelated to horizon-scale physics.

title: Non-Phantom Dark Energy
statement: Equation of state tends toward w=-1 from above (non-phantom) with possible mild evolution.
failure_condition: Phantom detection (w<-1) at any redshift.

title: Falsification Contract
statement: X1 claims have explicit falsification conditions stated in advance.

title: Active Cognitive Pruning Threshold

not_claimed: S_c depends on task and capacity.

title: Access-Network Collapse

title: Downstream Failure Rule

not_claimed: Not claiming specific architectures are/aren't agents.

statement: Self-verifying agency requires internal or coupled verification of action effects.
failure_condition: System classified self-verifying despite relying on external host for verification.

title: Misalignment is divergence between host and delegate action ...
statement: Misalignment is divergence between host and delegate action effects on boundary loss.
failure_condition: Divergent objectives do not produce divergent finite-difference action effects.

title: Culture and institutions are shared externalized distinction...
statement: Culture and institutions are shared externalized distinction infrastructures with verification costs.
failure_condition: Externalized symbols function semantically without interpreter or verification channel.

title: Attention as capacity-limited distinction admission into an ...
statement: Attention as capacity-limited distinction admission into an update channel.
dependencies: FDS core finite capacity; M0 attention definition
failure_condition: Attention-like selection shows no finite capacity, admission, or update gating.

title: Value and goal as boundary-relevance ranking under finite ca...
statement: Value and goal as boundary-relevance ranking under finite capacity.
dependencies: FDS core finite capacity; M0 value/goal definition
failure_condition: Value-like ranking shows no relation to causal boundary effects, costs, or horizons.

title: Meaning as actionable semantic quotient under finite capacit...
statement: Meaning as actionable semantic quotient under finite capacity.
dependencies: FDS core finite capacity; M0 meaning definition; M2 value-goal ranking
failure_condition: Compressed representations function semantically without preserving any action, prediction, verification, coordination, or boundary-relevant structure.

title: Coarse-grained anti-recurrence: capacity recovery is not dis...
statement: Coarse-grained anti-recurrence: capacity recovery is not distinction recovery.
dependencies: FDS core finite capacity; FDS finite projection
failure_condition: Exact preimage recovery from truncated record alone without side records or hidden inverse information.

title: Topological obstruction: some task distinctions are protecte...
statement: Topological obstruction: some task distinctions are protected against local forgetting by invariant structure.
dependencies: P4-001; invariant quotient map; NHSE model class
failure_condition: Invariant fails to suppress residual uncertainty, or local perturbation erases protected sector without protection-breaking event.

title: Speed-precision-dissipation bounds: boundary maintenance req...

failure_condition: Finite system maintains arbitrarily fast and precise boundary tasks at fixed capacity and fixed resource with no extra cost.

title: CKM CP-Phase Lower Bound
statement: For a CKM-type N×N unitary charged-current mixing matrix, an irreducible physical complex phase exists if and only if N≥3.
failure_condition: A CKM-like two-generation weak sector is shown to possess a physical irreducible CP phase.

title: Weak-Sector CP/T Orientation Bridge
statement: Weak-sector identity transformation requires a rephasing-invariant CP/T orientation.
failure_condition: A complete physical account of the audited microscopic weak identity-changing channel with no CP/T orientation, no compensating asymmetry, and no hidden orientation variable.

title: Weak Charged Current as Identity-Transformation Carrier
statement: The weak charged current is the Standard Model identity-transformation carrier.
failure_condition: Identity-changing weak-sector operations are realized by a different physical carrier outside the CKM charged current.

title: NCKM≥3 Conditional Theorem
statement: NCKM≥3 follows from the X2 chain: weak identity update → T/CP orientation → irreducible CKM phase → NCKM≥3.
failure_condition: Failure of any premise: the CP/T-orientation bridge, CPT assumptions, the weak-carrier mapping, or CKM phase counting.

title: Exactly Three Generations as Minimality Bridge
statement: Exactly three sequential chiral generations follow from minimality.
failure_condition: Discovery of a fourth sequential chiral generation with Standard Model gauge charges and weak charged-current participation, or a successful N>3 flavor architecture that reduces the relevant cost functional.

title: Nonzero Leptonic Dirac CP Phase
statement: If the lepton sector participates in the same CP/T-oriented identity-transformation requirement, and if the relevant observable channel is the Dirac PMNS phase, then X2 motivates a nonzero leptonic Dirac CP phase.
failure_condition: High-precision oscillation data establish δ_CP=0 or π under assumptions requiring leptonic CP/T-oriented identity transformation.
